Evidence About Contact Disclaimer Swiss site

Evidence status

Alzheimer's

Alzheimer's disease is associated with impaired cerebral glucose metabolism. Ketones may provide an alternative fuel signal, but clinical evidence remains early and heterogeneous.

Evidence review Emerging clinical evidence Last reviewed: May 26, 2026

Scientific rationale

  • Ketone bodies may partially bypass impaired glucose utilization and may also influence mitochondrial function, oxidative stress, inflammation, and synaptic signalling.
  • Interventions studied include ketogenic diets, MCT oil, ketone esters, and other exogenous ketone strategies, each with different adherence and safety profiles.

Research snapshot

  • Small clinical trials and reviews report cognitive or functional signals in some participants, but sample sizes, duration, outcome measures, and genetic factors vary.
  • The field still needs larger trials that distinguish symptomatic support from disease modification.

Clinical frame

  • Older adults with cognitive impairment are vulnerable to weight loss, medication complexity, and nutritional deficiency, so unsupervised restrictive diets can be risky.
  • Ketogenic strategies should not be presented as a proven dementia treatment.

Open questions

  • Which disease stage, APOE genotype, metabolic profile, or cognitive phenotype is most likely to respond?
  • Can benefits be achieved with less restrictive ketone strategies that preserve nutrition and quality of life?

Selected evidence

  1. Randomized crossover trial of a modified ketogenic diet in Alzheimer's disease Alzheimer's Research & Therapy, 2021
  2. Medium-chain triglycerides for the symptomatic treatment of dementia-related diseases Journal of Nutrition and Metabolism, 2024
  3. Consuming a modified Mediterranean ketogenic diet reverses the peripheral lipid signature of Alzheimer's disease in humans Alzheimer's & Dementia, 2025
  4. Ketogenic approaches for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease Current Opinion in Clinical Nutrition and Metabolic Care, 2024
  5. The impact of the ketogenic diet on Alzheimer's disease progression Nutrients, 2026
  6. Medium-chain triglycerides improve cognition and systemic metabolism in mouse models of Alzheimer's disease Molecular Neurodegeneration, 2025
